data_flow_trace.original: All 7 pending-pattern contracts call _requireInitialized() in their setForNextEpoch()
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: StakingConfig has been refactored to use the same pending-config pattern as its peers. setForNextEpoch() is now the single governance setter.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Caller must be GOVERNANCE AND contract must be initialized.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Issue targeted a state that no longer exists — StakingConfig already conforms to the peer pattern.

data_flow_trace.original: StakingConfig's four setters (setMinimumStake at line 89, setLockupDurationMicros 103, setUnbondingDelayMicros 121, setMinimumProposalStake 139) only check requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE) with no _requireInitialized().
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: The four per-field setters were entirely removed in commit 49dce4a ('refactor: apply pending config pattern to StakingConfig (GCC-004) (#33)', dated 2026-02-27). They are replaced by setForNextEpoch(uint256,uint64,uint64) which, at line 136, calls _requireInitialized() before mutating pending state. The guard implementation is at src/runtime/StakingConfig.sol:198-200 ('if (!_initialized) revert Errors.StakingConfigNotInitialized();').
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: setForNextEpoch now requires (a) requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE) at line 135, (b) _requireInitialized() at line 136, (c) _validateConfig(...) at line 137.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: The four targeted setters no longer exist — function signatures cited in the issue are gone from the current bytecode surface.

data_flow_trace.original: If GOVERNANCE were active pre-genesis, setMinimumStake(0) could write to uninitialized storage; a later initialize() would overwrite.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Even hypothetically, (a) setMinimumStake/setLockupDurationMicros/etc. no longer exist; (b) the replacement setForNextEpoch reverts with StakingConfigNotInitialized when _initialized == false; (c) _validateConfig also rejects _minimumStake == 0 via InvalidMinimumStake. The attack scenario has no remaining surface.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Any attempt to call setForNextEpoch prior to initialize() reverts on line 136.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Defense-in-depth gap has been closed by the refactor; StakingConfig now matches the peer pattern exactly.

reasoning_chain: The finding targets four specific setters (setMinimumStake, setLockupDurationMicros, setUnbondingDelayMicros, setMinimumProposalStake) that lack _requireInitialized(). These functions no longer exist in the current source — commit 49dce4a (#33, GCC-004) removed them and introduced the pending-config pattern. The replacement setForNextEpoch() at src/runtime/StakingConfig.sol:130-147 calls _requireInitialized() at line 136, which reverts with Errors.StakingConfigNotInitialized when _initialized is false (guard defined at lines 198-200). The test suite codifies this via test_RevertWhen_SetForNextEpoch_NotInitialized at test/unit/runtime/StakingConfig.t.sol:122-126. Step 2 of the attack is broken by the _requireInitialized() call at line 136, making the original vulnerability non-existent in current code.

key_evidence.why_matters: This is the only governance setter in current StakingConfig. It explicitly includes _requireInitialized() at line 136, directly closing the defense-in-depth gap raised by the finding.
The guard that actually enforces the initialization check — present and identical in form to peer config contracts.
Regression test locking in the behavior: a GOVERNANCE-originated call pre-initialize must revert with StakingConfigNotInitialized.

counterargument: One could argue the finding is PARTIAL rather than NOT_EXISTS if one considers the historical code that existed at the time of audit. Additionally, one might worry that other governance-mutable state (e.g., hasPendingConfig written indirectly) still lacks protection pre-init. However, reading the current file end-to-end shows every non-view external function (initialize at line 86-101 with AlreadyInitialized check, setForNextEpoch at 130-147 with _requireInitialized, applyPendingConfig at 156-171 with _requireInitialized) is gated. No writable surface remains that could be reached pre-init. The finding's scope is explicitly 'StakingConfig setters missing _requireInitialized() guard' — those setters do not exist, so the vulnerability as described is absent. A reviewer rebutting NOT_EXISTS would need to point to a currently-present external function that writes state without an init check, and there is none.
